summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting for regular business on July 6, 2021. Major topics included an update on the criminal sentencing of Donnie Combs to four years in prison for eight felony charges related to illegal burning, dumping, and operating an illegal solid waste facility, with civil proceedings still pending. The board approved the 2021 street resurfacing bid, road striping, a waste/recycling contract with Rumpke, a postage meter lease, the 2022 tax budget, and accepted a fire captain's resignation due to disability. The board also entered executive session to discuss an appointment.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (June 23 – July 6, 2021) – passed
Motion to increase appropriations by $24,500 for 2021 striping project – passed
Motion to award 2021 street resurfacing bid to Jurgensen's in the amount of $116,394 – passed
Motion to approve road edge striping, lane striping, and stop/arrow markings by Arrow Mark Company in the amount of $7,446 – passed
Motion to accept resignation of fire and EMS captain effective retroactive to June 29, 2021 – passed
Motion to approve 2021-2022 property casualty insurance for Goshen Park District paid to Island Insurance/The Ohio Plan in the amount of $2,408 – passed
Motion to approve the 2022 tax budget – passed
Motion to authorize township administrator to enter 36-month contract with Rumpke Waste and Recycling Services for township dumpster locations – passed
Motion to authorize township administrator to enter 60-month contract with Quadient Leasing for postage meter at $73.26/month – passed
Motion to enter executive session to discuss appointment – passed
Motion to adjourn at 8:04 PM – passed
action_items: Township administrator and Chad to work on OWPC state grant application for road infrastructure projects
Set up online property maintenance complaint form for residents
Melissa to continue posting updates to the township website
Plan recognition event for retiring fire and EMS captain
Push information about Chamber of Commerce appreciation banquet (August 26) to board and administrator
Pursue civil case against Donnie Combs for asset seizure and site cleanup
Gene Schmidt presentation to be scheduled for August 10 meeting
Service department to continue shed construction project at fire station
key_amounts: $116,394 – 2021 street resurfacing bid awarded to Jurgensen's
$7,446 – road edge striping and lane markings by Arrow Mark Company
$2,408 – property casualty insurance for Goshen Park District
$1.5 million – civil fine owed by Donnie Combs to the State of Ohio
$3 million – estimated cleanup cost for Combs contaminated sites
$1,300 – estimated savings over 3 years with Rumpke waste contract vs. Republic
$73.26/month – postage meter lease rate locked in for 60 months
notable_topics: Donnie Combs criminal sentencing – 4 years prison for 8 felony charges (illegal burning, dumping, illegal solid waste facility); civil case pending for $1.5M fine and asset seizure
OWPC state grant application for road infrastructure
Online property maintenance complaint system for residents
New Officer Nick Culver and Officer Groves joining police department
Resignation of fire and EMS captain due to disability
Postage meter lease with Quadient Leasing
Goshen Park District insurance and kickball tournament
notes: The Donnie Combs case discussion was a significant moment – the township had been involved for 13+ years pursuing criminal and civil action. Combs pleaded guilty to all 8 felonies and was sentenced to 4 years (minimum 6 months). The state lawyer praised the township's role in supporting the prosecution. Trustee Allen was absent due to vacation. The August 10 meeting was clarified as the next regular meeting after schedule adjustments. Executive session was held to discuss an appointment but no action was taken publicly afterward.
title: Spc Mtg BOT 7621
